summary:
You are given an m x n grid of rooms. Each cell is one of three things: -1 marks a wall (or obstacle), 0 marks a gate, and 2147483647 (the maximum 32-bit integer, used as infinity) marks an empty room.

Fill each empty room with the distance to its nearest gate, measured in single up/down/left/right steps. If no gate can reach a room, leave that cell as infinity. Modify the grid in place.

examples.input: rooms = [[INF, -1, 0, INF], [INF, INF, INF, -1], [INF, -1, INF, -1], [0, -1, INF, INF]]
examples.explanation: INF stands for 2147483647. Each empty room is replaced by the number of steps to the closest gate, with walls (-1) blocking movement.
A single gate; there are no empty rooms to fill.

constraints: Movement is restricted to the four cardinal directions; walls cannot be passed through
Rooms unreachable from any gate keep the value 2147483647
